title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: mqriskR package provides functions actuarial risk modeling, including survival models, insurance annuity values, premium calculations, reserves, multiple-decrement models, mortality improvement projections. functions designed align standard actuarial notation support teaching, exam preparation, reproducible actuarial analysis. vignette provides brief introduction main functionality package. detailed derivations additional examples, see Models Quantifying Risk.

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: begin computing survival probabilities simple model. gives probability life aged 40 survives 10 years uniform distribution deaths limiting age 100.
code: library(mqriskR)  # Probability of surviving 10 years from age 40 tpx(10, x = 40, model = "uniform", omega = 100) #> [1] 0.8333333

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: next compute insurance actuarial present values. first value actuarial present value whole life insurance paying 1 end year death, second value present value 10-year term insurance pays 1 death occurs within term.
code: # Whole life insurance Ax(40, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100) #> [1] 0.3154882  # 10-year term insurance Axn1(40, n = 10, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100) #> [1] 0.1286956

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: now compute annuity values. first value represents present value whole life annuity-immediate, second gives present value 10-year temporary annuity-immediate.
code: # Whole life annuity-immediate ax(40, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100) #> [1] 13.37475  # 10-year temporary annuity axn(40, n = 10, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100) #> [1] 7.065505

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: Using equivalence principle, compute level annual net premium. level annual net premium calculated using equivalence principle, expected present value benefits equals expected present value premiums. corresponds identity Px:n¯|1=Ax:n¯|1/̈x:n¯|. P_{x:\overline{n}|}^{1} = A_{x:\overline{n}|}^{1} / \ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|}.
code: Ax_term <- Axn1(40, n = 10, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100) adotx_term <- adotxn(40, n = 10, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100)  premium <- Ax_term / adotx_term premium #> [1] 0.01703695

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: now compute simple prospective reserve time t=5t = 5 contract. prospective reserve time t=5t = 5, representing expected future loss time based remaining benefits premiums. illustrates standard prospective reserve formula: tVx:n¯|1=Ax+t:n−t¯|1−Px:n¯|1äx+t:n−t¯|. {}_{t}V_{x:\overline{n}|}^{1} = A_{x+t:\overline{n-t}|}^{1} - P_{x:\overline{n}|}^{1} \ddot{}_{x+t:\overline{n-t}|}.
code: t <- 5  # Prospective reserve: V_t = A_{x+t:n-t} - P * ä_{x+t:n-t} Ax_future <- Axn1(40 + t, n = 10 - t, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100) adotx_future <- adotxn(40 + t, n = 10 - t, i = 0.05, model = "uniform", omega = 100)  V_t <- Ax_future - premium * adotx_future V_t #> [1] 0.003947702

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: package supports multiple-decrement tables. table summarizes multiple-decrement model, including cause-specific decrement probabilities, total decrement probabilities, survival probabilities, number lives remaining age. can compute survival probabilities: gives probability life aged 46 remains force 3 years causes decrement considered.

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: package also includes mortality improvement projections. gives projected one-year death probability allowing mortality improvement base year projection year.

title: Getting Started with mqriskR
text: mqriskR package provides unified framework actuarial modeling, covering survival models, insurance annuity functions, premium calculations, reserves, multiple-decrement models, mortality improvement. Users can combine functions build complex actuarial models transparent reproducible way.

title: Annual annuity functions (Chapter 8) — annuity_annual
text: Annual whole life, temporary, deferred, actuarial accumulated value annuity functions immediate, due, continuous forms. Computes annual whole life annuity-immediate \(a_x = \sum_{t=1}^{\infty} v^t \, {}_t p_x\). Computes annual whole life annuity-due \(\ddot{}_x = \sum_{t=0}^{\infty} v^t \, {}_t p_x = 1 + a_x\). Computes continuous whole life annuity \(\bar{}_x = \int_0^{\infty} v^t \, {}_t p_x \, dt\). Computes annual temporary annuity-immediate \(a_{x:\overline{n}|} = \sum_{t=1}^{n} v^t \, {}_t p_x\). Computes annual temporary annuity-due \(\ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|} = \sum_{t=0}^{n-1} v^t \, {}_t p_x\). Computes continuous temporary annuity \(\bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|} = \int_0^n v^t \, {}_t p_x \, dt\). Computes annual deferred whole life annuity-immediate \({}_{n|}a_x = {}_nE_x \, a_{x+n}\). Computes annual deferred whole life annuity-due \({}_{n|}\ddot{}_x = {}_nE_x \, \ddot{}_{x+n}\). Computes continuous deferred whole life annuity \({}_{n|}\bar{}_x = {}_nE_x \, \bar{}_{x+n}\). Computes \(s_{x:\overline{n}|} = a_{x:\overline{n}|} / {}_nE_x\). Computes \(\ddot{s}_{x:\overline{n}|} = \ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|} / {}_nE_x\). Computes \(\bar{s}_{x:\overline{n}|} = \bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|} / {}_nE_x\).

title: UDD annuity approximations — annuity_approximations_udd
text: UDD-based approximations Chapter 8 annuity functions. Computes $$\ddot{}_x^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)\ddot{}_x - \beta(m).$$ Computes $$\ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|}^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)\ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|} - \beta(m)(1-{}_nE_x).$$ Computes $${}_{n\mid}\ddot{}_x^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)\,{}_{n\mid}\ddot{}_x - \beta(m)\,{}_nE_x.$$ Computes $$a_x^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)a_x + \gamma(m).$$ Computes $$a_{x:\overline{n}|}^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)a_{x:\overline{n}|} + \gamma(m)(1-{}_nE_x).$$ Computes $${}_{n\mid}a_x^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)\,{}_{n\mid}a_x + \gamma(m)\,{}_nE_x.$$ Computes $$\ddot{s}_{x:\overline{n}|}^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)\ddot{s}_{x:\overline{n}|} - \beta(m)\left(\frac{1}{{}_nE_x}-1\right).$$ Computes $$s_{x:\overline{n}|}^{(m)} \approx \alpha(m)s_{x:\overline{n}|} + \gamma(m)\left(\frac{1}{{}_nE_x}-1\right).$$ Computes $$\bar{}_x \approx \frac{id}{\delta^2}\ddot{}_x - \frac{-\delta}{\delta^2}.$$ Uses identity $$\bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|} \approx \frac{1-\bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|}}{\delta}$$ together package's existing Chapter 7 UDD insurance approximation \(\bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|}\). Computes $${}_{n\mid}\bar{}_x \approx {}_nE_x \, \bar{}_{x+n}.$$

Annuity-insurance relationships (Chapter 8) — annuity_relationships
text: file provides core Chapter 8 identities linking annual continuous annuity functions corresponding insurance functions. Computes \(a_x = (v - A_x)/d\). Computes \(\ddot{}_x = (1 - A_x)/d\). Computes \(\bar{}_x = (1 - \bar{}_x)/\delta\). Computes \(a_{x:\overline{n}|} = \ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|} - 1 + {}_nE_x\) together \(\ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|} = (1 - A_{x:\overline{n}|})/d\). Computes \(\ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|} = (1 - A_{x:\overline{n}|})/d\). Computes \(\bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|} = (1 - \bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|})/\delta\). Computes \({}_{n|}a_x = {}_nE_x\, a_{x+n}\). Computes \({}_{n|}\ddot{}_x = {}_nE_x\, \ddot{}_{x+n}\). Computes \({}_{n|}\bar{}_x = {}_nE_x\, \bar{}_{x+n}\).

title: Annuity-insurance relationships (Chapter 8) — annuity_relationships
text: Included identities: whole life immediate: \(a_x = (v - A_x)/d\) whole life due: \(\ddot{}_x = (1 - A_x)/d\) whole life continuous: \(\bar{}_x = (1 - \bar{}_x)/\delta\) temporary immediate: \(a_{x:\overline{n}|} = (1 - A_{x:\overline{n}|})/d - 1 + {}_nE_x\) temporary due: \(\ddot{}_{x:\overline{n}|} = (1 - A_{x:\overline{n}|})/d\) temporary continuous: \(\bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|} = (1 - \bar{}_{x:\overline{n}|})/\delta\) deferred immediate: \({}_{n\mid}a_x = {}_nE_x a_{x+n}\) deferred due: \({}_{n\mid}\ddot{}_x = {}_nE_x \ddot{}_{x+n}\) deferred continuous: \({}_{n\mid}\bar{}_x = {}_nE_x \bar{}_{x+n}\) wrapper functions evaluate Chapter 8 relationships using Chapter 7 insurance functions already implemented package. Hence \(a_{x:\overline{n}|} = (1 - A_{x:\overline{n}|})/d - 1 + {}_nE_x\).

title: Varying-payment annuity functions (Chapter 8) — annuity_varying_payments
text: Chapter 8 non-level annuity functions increasing decreasing life annuities. Computes $$(Ia)_x = \sum_{t=1}^{\infty} t \, v^t \, {}_tp_x.$$ Computes $$(Ia)_{x:\overline{n}|} = \sum_{t=1}^{n} t \, v^t \, {}_tp_x.$$ Computes $$(Da)_{x:\overline{n}|} = \sum_{t=1}^{n} (n+1-t)\, v^t \, {}_tp_x.$$ Computes $$(\ddot{})_x = \sum_{t=0}^{\infty} (t+1)\, v^t \, {}_tp_x.$$ Computes $$(\ddot{})_{x:\overline{n}|} = \sum_{t=0}^{n-1} (t+1)\, v^t \, {}_tp_x.$$ Computes $$(D\ddot{})_{x:\overline{n}|} = \sum_{t=0}^{n-1} (n-t)\, v^t \, {}_tp_x.$$ Computes $$(\bar{}\bar{})_x = \int_0^\infty t\,v^t\,{}_tp_x\,dt.$$ Computes $$(\bar{}\bar{})_{x:\overline{n}|} = \int_0^n t\,v^t\,{}_tp_x\,dt.$$ Computes $$(\bar{D}\bar{})_{x:\overline{n}|} = \int_0^n (n-t)\,v^t\,{}_tp_x\,dt.$$

title: Spot-rate actuarial present value functions — chapter15_spot_interest_apv
text: Chapter 15 functions actuarial present values discounting uses spot rates maturity. \(z_t\) denotes annual effective spot rate maturity \(t\), discount factor \((1+z_t)^{-t}\). Computes $$ {}_nE_x = (1+z_n)^{-n}\cdot {}_np_x. $$ Computes $$ A_{x:\overline{n}|}^1 = \sum_{t=1}^{n}(1+z_t)^{-t}\cdot {}_{t-1}p_x \cdot q_{x+t-1}. $$ Computes $$ A_{x:\overline{n}|} = A_{x:\overline{n}|}^1 + {}_nE_x $$ using spot-rate discount factors. immediate annuity, $$ a_{x:\overline{n}|} = \sum_{t=1}^{n}(1+z_t)^{-t}\cdot {}_tp_x. $$

text: Chapter 15 functions actuarial present values variable annual effective interest rates interpreted yearly scenario \(i_1, i_2, \dots, i_n\). Computes APV \(n\)-year pure endowment variable annual interest scenario: $$ {}_nE_x = v_n \cdot {}_np_x. $$ Computes APV \(n\)-year term insurance benefit paid end year death variable annual interest scenario: $$ A_{x:\overline{n}|}^1 = \sum_{t=1}^{n} v_t \cdot {}_{t-1}p_x \cdot q_{x+t-1}. $$ Computes APV \(n\)-year endowment insurance variable annual interest scenario: $$ A_{x:\overline{n}|} = A_{x:\overline{n}|}^1 + {}_nE_x. $$ Computes APV \(n\)-year temporary life annuity variable annual interest scenario.

title: Survival models (Chapter 5): core survival functions + parametric models — survival_models
text: ## Age--failure random variable (T0) - `S0(t, ...)` : survival function  S0(t) = Pr(T0 > t) - `F0(t, ...)` : CDF              F0(t) = Pr(T0 <= t) = 1 - S0(t) - `f0(t, ...)` : density          f0(t) = d/dt F0(t) - `hazard0(t, ...)` : hazard      lambda0(t) = f0(t) / S0(t) - `cumhaz0(t, ...)` : cum hazard  Lambda0(t) = \(\int_0^t \lambda_0(y) dy\) ## Conditional (future lifetime) random variable (Tx), given alive age x - `tpx(t, x, ...)` :  \({}_t p_x = S0(x+t)/S0(x)\) - `tqx(t, x, ...)` :  \({}_t q_x = 1 - {}_t p_x\) - `fx(t, x, ...)`  :  \(f_x(t) = f0(x+t)/S0(x)\) ## Expectations - `ex_complete(x, ...)` : \(\overset{\circ}{e}_x = \int_0^\infty {}_t p_x\,dt\) - `ex_curtate(x, ...)`  : \(e_x = E[K_x] = \sum_{k\ge 1} {}_k p_x\) (truncated sum) ## Models supported - `"uniform"`     (de Moivre)      parameter: `omega` - `"exponential"` (constant force) parameter: `lambda` - `"gompertz"`                  parameters: `B`, `c` - `"makeham"`                   parameters: ``, `B`, `c` - `"weibull"`                   parameters: `shape`, `scale` functions vectorized `t` (`x` applicable).
